item.title: Crit Lives Podcast
item.content:encoded: Hello Mew and Attila! I have selected your SCP for the next episode of Crit Lives Within. The publication should be out tonight, 6/30, so stay tuned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I enjoy writing stories that stand on their own a bit, but are still enjoyable. I’m glad I could get that across! (With Attila’s help polishing of course lol)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed. Not everything needs to be a world-ending threat or an important Foundation trophy. I quite enjoy these small-scale stories.

Edit: meant to be a reply to DrSevere

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Omg thank you sooo much. You have no idea what this means to me. This was one of my first ideas, too. Before I really got much into SCP writing. While the original idea was much different, the idea of a bag showing personalized items was what I had, and reviewers could work with it all because of one simple log: the log about Gates. The story of this SCP is long and complicated for such a simple outcome, and I’m really glad you enjoyed it! It really means a lot! Heh I got sappy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! It means a lot to me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the kind of SCP I'm going to use as an example for new writers to help them understand what their goal should be with their first SCP. No crazy world-ending anomalies, no X-men, no million crosslinks, just a simple anomaly with a simple effect that tells an incredibly human story. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so so much! That means a lot to me!

The reason we didn’t add more test logs was cause we felt we didn’t really need them. Most of our reviewers told us we didn’t need a bunch cause the focus was Gates. We only needed enough to tell the story! Thanks for your input, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! To me this is a great example of a simple concept with a lot of room for depth and speculation into its exact nature.
I also like the little touch of the researcher noting that they're not certain if it's based on alternate history or a cognitohazard - that little touch of in-universe uncertainty made it a lot more real and engaging to me, and was a nice setup for the interview.

Would have liked more test logs, though - still gets a comfy +1 from me :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! It means so so much you have no idea! And I’ll fix that right up! Thanks for pointing it out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the transition from the test logs into the story, the escalation was good for a short article. Weird typo in the interview near the end "We don't what could have been different." I don't know what this was supposed to say but it doesn't seem right. But yeah +1!

item.title: Co-author post
item.content:encoded:
"For of all sad words of tongue or pen, The saddest are these: 'It might have been!'"
-John Greenleaf Whittier

This was a lot of fun to help write. Thanks to Mew-ltiverse for coming up with the idea and letting me tag along!

The image is from https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Carpetbag_MET_63.32.46_CP4.jpg.

item.title: Author Post!
item.content:encoded: So many people fuckin helped critique this thing I swear it was half the site lol. But for real, I don’t even feel like coding the names so Ihp, Ayers, TheBlueHour, RadioactiveRADS, Caspin2, UraniumEmpire, TheMightyMcB, and anyone else I forgot to thank because so many fucking people helped. And BIG thanks to Attila, because well— he’s my co-author! I was desperate, at the happiest place on earth, Disney World down in the dumps (so bad my family noticed, I had to continually convince them I was fine) because a piece I’d been working on for months was failing. I was eating dinner, and I pulled out my phone, and saw a forum thread I posted calling out for help got attention by Attila the Pun who said he’d co-author the article with me. My spirits lifted. Seriously Attila, you don’t know how greatful I am for your help. We both worked this week to pick this piece back up, and here it is! Thank you so much!
